name: Generate short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive end to end

parameters.text: =✅ Generate short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive end to end — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 12 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Content Calendar (Google Sheets)

### Read Content Calendar (Google Sheets)

Reads every row from 'Asset Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Due for Publishing? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Due for Publishing?

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Post One at a Time

### Post One at a Time

Loop controller — releases 1 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Count Published · ②→ Publish to Slack

nodes.name: Note: Post One at a Time

parameters.content:
### Publish to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#media`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Space Out Posts · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Space Out Posts

Pauses 30 seconds — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Mark Row Published

### Mark Row Published

Upserts a row into 'Asset Tracker', matched on `assetId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Post One at a Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Count Published

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Sort Assets Newest First

### Sort Assets Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Asset Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#media`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#media`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Generate short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive end to end

**Pattern:** Video Production · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Generate short videos with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive end to end. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual editing is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Read Content Calendar (Google Sheets) → Due for Publishing? → Post One at a Time → Count Published → Save Execution Metadata → Sort Assets Newest First → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to video production without manual effort
- Anyone automating editing in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Standardizes the process every time
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Posts go out one at a time through a batch-of-1 loop with a Wait between each, not all at once — most social APIs rate-limit bursts, and spacing also just reads as more natural than five posts landing in the same second. A row is marked published only after the post succeeds, so a mid-run crash resumes cleanly instead of re-posting everything.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Change the spacing delay and see how total run time scales with calendar size.
2. Add a second platform and post to both for each due item.
3. Manually mark one row as already-published and confirm the filter skips it.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your editing process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
